title: Epipactis minor
intro: Epipactis minor, commonly known as the small helleborine, is a perennial orchid species native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its delicate white flowers and slender stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Epipactis minor include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Epipactis minor is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory disorders.
taxonomy: Epipactis minor belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family comprises over 30,000 known species of orchids, making it the largest family of flowering plants. Within the family Orchidaceae, Epipactis minor is classified as a member of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Epipactis is a group of terrestrial orchids, and Epipactis minor is one of the species within this group. The classification of Epipactis minor reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other orchid species.
